news.headline: Lexeo Therapeutics CEO, CFO and COO Sell Shares to Cover RSU Tax Withholding
news.summary: Following performance RSU vesting on June 29, CFO Louis Tamayo sold 4,641 shares at a weighted-average price of $4.49, CEO R. Nolan Townsend sold 25,947 shares at $4.48, and COO Jose Manuel Otero sold 4,666 shares at roughly $4.48, all routine transactions to cover tax obligations with each executive retaining substantial remaining holdings.

news.headline: JPMorgan Cuts Lexeo Therapeutics Price Target to $9 from $12, Keeps Overweight
news.summary: JPMorgan lowered its price target on Lexeo to $9 from $12 while maintaining an Overweight rating, citing an updated valuation model following recent clinical and regulatory developments. Other analysts remain more bullish in the same period: Raymond James reiterated Strong Buy with a $25 target, Oppenheimer kept Outperform at $20 citing LX2006 as a '$1B+ US peak sales opportunity,' and Cantor Fitzgerald held Overweight at $19.

news.headline: Lexeo Shareholders Reelect Directors, Ratify KPMG as Auditor at Annual Meeting
news.summary: At its virtual 2026 annual meeting, Lexeo stockholders reelected three Class III directors, including CEO R. Nolan Townsend, and ratified KPMG LLP as the company's independent auditor for fiscal 2026.

news.headline: Lexeo Therapeutics Announces Publication in JAMA Cardiology of Phase I/II Data for LX2006 in Friedreich Ataxia
news.summary: Pooled data from 17 Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy patients across two independent studies of LX2006 showed a 28% mean improvement in left ventricular mass index (LVMI) at 6 months and 33% at 12 months among patients with abnormal baseline LVMI, with a generally well-tolerated safety profile, as published in JAMA Cardiology. The results reinforce Lexeo's rationale for advancing LX2006 into its pivotal trial.

news.headline: Lexeo Therapeutics Announces Regulatory Update and Registrational Trial Design for LX2006 Gene Therapy in Friedreich Ataxia
news.summary: Lexeo finalized the protocol and statistical analysis plan for SUNRISE-FA 2, a pivotal open-label trial of high-dose LX2006 in Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy, after the FDA confirmed no additional nonclinical bridging studies are required and cleared use of the company's optimized Sf9-baculovirus commercial-scale manufacturing process. Lexeo guided to enrollment beginning by end of June 2026, a topline readout in 2H 2027, and a BLA submission in 1H 2028 under the accelerated approval pathway; shares jumped nearly 13% on the news, and H.C. Wainwright raised its price target to $15 from $9 citing improved approval odds and an accelerated 2028 launch timeline.

catalysts.label: SUNRISE-FA 2 first patient dosing / trial initiation update
catalysts.detail: Lexeo guided that SUNRISE-FA 2 enrollment was on track to begin by the end of June 2026 following finalization of the pivotal trial protocol and FDA confirmation that no additional bridging studies are required; as of July 23, 2026 the company had not yet publicly confirmed that dosing of the first patient has occurred.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: Lexeo has not yet officially confirmed its Q2 2026 report date as of July 23, 2026; prior-year cadence (Q2 2025 results reported August 14, 2025; Q2 2024 results reported August 12, 2024) and its Q1 2026 report date of May 11, 2026 point to a mid-August 2026 release.

catalysts.label: LX2020 12-month high-dose cohort data update (PKP2-ACM)
catalysts.detail: Lexeo plans to report 12-month follow-up data for all high-dose participants in the HEROIC-PKP2 Phase 1/2 trial of LX2020 in PKP2-associated arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy, building on the January 2026 interim readout (93% and 162% mean PKP2 protein increases in low- and high-dose cohorts); Oppenheimer has flagged this readout as a key upcoming catalyst.

catalysts.label: FDA regulatory engagement on LX2020/PKP2-ACM registrational path
catalysts.detail: Company has guided to further FDA discussions in 2026 regarding a potential registrational path for LX2020.

catalysts.label: SUNRISE-FA 2 topline efficacy readout (LX2006)
catalysts.detail: Topline 6-month efficacy data (left ventricular mass index) from the pivotal SUNRISE-FA 2 trial of LX2006 in Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy is expected in the second half of 2027.

catalysts.label: Planned BLA submission for LX2006
catalysts.detail: Lexeo intends to submit a Biologics License Application to the FDA seeking accelerated approval of LX2006 for Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy, contingent on SUNRISE-FA 2 results.

pulse: Lexeo's lead program, LX2006 for Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy, gained momentum in mid-to-late June 2026 as the company finalized its pivotal SUNRISE-FA 2 trial design with FDA input, published supportive Phase I/II data in JAMA Cardiology (28-33% mean LVMI improvement), and saw shareholders reelect the board and ratify KPMG as auditor at the June 25 annual meeting; H.C. Wainwright raised its target to $15 on the FDA news even as JPMorgan cut its target to $9, leaving analysts split alongside more bullish Oppenheimer ($20), Cantor Fitzgerald ($19) and Raymond James ($25) calls. News flow has remained quiet through all of July 2026 to date, with the last notable item being routine Form 4 sales by the CEO, CFO and COO on July 1 to cover RSU tax withholding and no further material company announcements as of July 23, 2026; the stock has traded in a narrow roughly $4.5-$4.9 band since mid-June. Investors are now watching for confirmation that SUNRISE-FA 2 has begun dosing patients (guided for around mid-2026/Q3 2026), an unconfirmed Q2 2026 earnings report expected mid-August 2026, and the earlier-stage PKP2-ACM program (LX2020), whose next major catalyst is a 12-month high-dose data update expected in Q4 2026.
